title: 2024 in review
summary: See our full annual report for a more in depth summary of temperature, precipitation, and notable weather events in 2024. At the global scale, 2024 was the warmest year on record in the ERA5 reanalysis as per the Copernicus Climate Change Service , breaking the previous record from 2023. Like in 2023, mean temperatures in 2024 in Alaska were above average but not record-breaking. Alaska was moderately warmer than the 1991-2020 normal with a deviation of 0.9°F ( NCEI statewide temperature timeseries ). All of the Alaska climate divisions were also above average, with highest positive deviations on the North Slope. Mean annual (2024) air temperature deviations (in Fahrenheit) from the normal (1991 - 2020) for selected stations. Overlay: ERA5 gridded temperature data, courtesy of Copernicus.eu. Statewide mean annual air temperature deviations from the 1991-2020 climate normal (°F). Data source: NOAA NCEI Temperature i...
